hypothesis: The plain `adaptive_budget_topk` failure suggests dynamic support allocation needs a stronger representational backbone, not that the mechanism is useless; adding adaptive-budget selection on top of the proven low-rank residual trunk may produce a better K=24 tradeoff than either plain adaptive-budget or the recent soft-codebook branches.
summary: Severe stagnation is active and recent fundamentally different probes have still lost, so the next step should be a new architecture rather than another recipe tweak. Add and test `lowrank_adaptive_budget_residual` under `sparsify/` only: reuse the winning low-rank residual trunk, replace fixed per-sample TopK selection with an adaptive-budget selector, and evaluate it first at the valuable `K=24` anchor with the stable EF=12/signum/LR=3.2e-3 recipe. This is one clean architectural hypothesis: dynamic budget allocation may only become competitive once paired with the stronger low-rank decomposition.
self_review: This follows the severe-stagnation policy by making a fundamentally different architecture move instead of another scalar adjustment. It is higher risk than reusing an existing family, but the search has already shown that plain dynamic-budget selection and several recent soft-codebook variants are not enough on their own; combining the adaptive selector with the strongest low-rank backbone is the most defensible next unknown to test.
notes_to_memory: Round 108 showed plain `adaptive_budget_topk` is a catastrophic miss at `K=24`, so any further dynamic-budget exploration should only continue if paired with a substantially stronger backbone.
Round 109 should test a new family `lowrank_adaptive_budget_residual` that keeps the proven low-rank residual trunk and changes only the support-allocation mechanism to adaptive budget.

Because this introduces a new architecture string, complete the full registration chain and exact smoke test before training: whitelist, factory dispatch, concrete class, then instantiate/forward/backward with the exact launch string.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_adaptive_budget_residual` is competitive at `K=24`, run the same fixed recipe at `K=16` or `K=32` to map whether the gain is localized or broad across the mid/low-K band.
If it runs cleanly but clearly loses at `K=24`, treat dynamic-budget routing as unpromising even with a strong low-rank backbone and move to a different mechanism entirely.
If the new family fails at sanity or first backward, repair the integration/runtime issue before drawing any scientific conclusion about the architecture.
